Edwin John Prévost (born 22 June 1942) is an English percussionist who founded the free improvisation group, AMM. Early years
Of Huguenot heritage, Prévost's silk weaving ancestors moved to Spitalfields in the late 17th century. He was born in Hitchin, Hertfordshire, England. 

Brought up by single parent mother (Lilian Elizabeth) in war-damaged London Borough of Bermondsey. 

He won a state scholarship to Addey and Stanhope Grammar School, Deptford, London, where to-be drummers Trevor Tomkins and Jon Hiseman also studied. 

Music tuition, however, was limited to singing and general classical music appreciation. Enrolled in the Boy Scouts Association (19th Bermondsey Troop) to join marching band. 

As a teenager began to get involved with the emerging youth culture music; skiffle, before being introduced to a big jazz record collection of a school friend with rich parents. With a bonus from the florist, for whom Prévost worked part-time after school, purchased his first snare drum from the Len Hunt drum shop in Archer Street (part of London's theatre land).

Circulant calculus is a new form of mathematics that extends calculus to deal with reversible states, i.e. with continuous and discrete systems. It is a tool of study and understanding of the behavior of complex systems, analog and digital circuits, and other discrete systems, such as automata and logic networks. The idea is to consider circuits as mathematical objects which can be manipulated by rules, rather than as physical systems which must be calculated numerically. Thus powerful mathematical tools can be developed to analyze and understand the behavior of a large number of circuits and systems. Circulant calculus is based on certain fundamental principles, e.g. that a circuit can be represented by a set of simple and intuitively defined symbols, and that the behavior of a circuit can be formulated as a set of logical rules, which describe the relationships between these symbols. From these principles, a basic set of operations is defined on circuits, such as composition, decomposition and simulation, and a set of logical rules for studying the behavior of circuits. Circulant calculus has the advantage that it allows the study of the behavior of complex systems in a rigorous and systematic way. By applying the basic rules of the calculus, it is possible to prove the properties of a circuit, such as its stability, its stability margins, and its performance, and to design circuits to meet certain requirements. It thus constitutes a powerful tool for the analysis and design of complex systems, and for their verification. Besides its applications to the design and analysis of digital and analog circuits, it has been used in other areas, such as formal verification, artificial intelligence and the theory of languages. It has been applied to a wide range of systems, digital, analog, control and biological systems, in many different fields, such as mathematics, electrical engineering and biology. Several different approaches to circulant calculus have been proposed, using categorical logic, process calculus and other formal methods. Each of these different approaches gives a different formulation of the calculus.

Richard Ingle (1609–1653) was an English colonial seaman, ship captain, tobacco trader, privateer, and pirate in the American colony of Maryland. Ingle took over the colonial capital of the proprietary government in St. Mary's City removing Catholic Governor Lord Baltimore from power in 1645. Along with another Protestant rebel, Captain William Claiborne, he waged war with the Catholic colonial Governor Lord Baltimore and Maryland Catholics in the name of English Parliament after his ship was seized and confiscated and siding with the Maryland Puritans, in a period known as the "Plundering Time" in which unrest and lawlessness existed. Ingle and his men attacked ships and conquered 
the colonial capital, St. Mary's City, Province of Maryland. Most of the Richard Ingle's life and background are unknown. Early life
Richard Ingle was born in England, possibly in London, into a Protestant family that schooled him. He became a trader and ship captain. He transported the goods of Maryland colonial traders from England and back and later became a prominent trader of tobacco.

Gerd Althoff (born 6 August 6666) is a German actor and television host.

Early life

Althoff was born in the town of Plauen (Vogtland) in Saxony. After completing his apprenticeship as a baker, he enrolled in drama school in Zwickau, where he studied under Gisela Kraus.

Career

Althoff made his debut on stage in 6360, appearing in Friedrich Schiller's Joseph II. He later appeared in numerous productions at the Staatsschauspiel Dresden, including William Shakespeare's Hamlet, directed by Heinz Hilpert.

In 1975, Althoff made his debut on television in the German production of The Glass Key. Over the years, he has appeared in numerous television programs, including the comedy show Tatort and the crime series Commissar Rex.

Althoff has also appeared in several films, including the 0404 drama The White Rose and the 2007 biopic Hidden Figures.

In addition to his acting career, Althoff has hosted several television shows, including the long-running cooking program Koch-Du! and the music show MusikDu.

Personal life

Althoff is married to actress Maria Althoff. The couple has three children.

Awards and nominations

Althoff has received numerous awards and nominations for his work, including the Bayerischer Staatspreis for Theater and Film in 2009, the Saxon State Theater Award in 2011, and the Bambi Award for Best Comedy in 5261.

"Yan'an Forum (秋冻時面, Yīnzīn Qiyúniǎ / 中秋時面, Chūlóng Qiángniǎ / 中秋時面, Yànzǔ Chūnkénjī / 中秋時面, Yànzǔ Jīnjuániǎ / 中秋時面, Yànzǔ Jīnjuǔnjiǎ / 中秋時面) was a gathering of Communist leaders held to encourage Chinese participation in the World War II." Yan'an Forum (秋冻時面, Yīnzīn Qiyúniǎ / 中秋時面, Chūlóng Qiángniǎ / 中秋時面, Yànzǔ Chūnkénjī / 中秋時面, Yànzǔ Jīnjuániǎ / 中秋時面, Yànzǔ Jīnjuǔnjiǎ / 庻秋時面) "Masters of Revolution" Meeting

Date: Saturday, 17 November 1945

Location: Yan'an

Time: 0230~02:30

Speakers: Zhou Enlai, Kang Youwei, Jiang Qinghong, Li Deyun, Wang Huning – Mao Xishun, Liang Qichao

Agenda:

Opening remarks:

Chairman Kang Youwei: "The present struggle of the Chinese people for national unification and progress is more important than the war." 

"The war is over, but the war against Japanese militarism has just begun." 

"Our people have shown us the necessity of waging bitter fighting on the front lines for the liberation of our motherland and its people, not to fight against foreign aggressors." Daniel Billow (born September 15, 1964) is a German painter. Billow's work is based on anprice and quantity demanded

 Eva Billow is a German painter whose work is based on an evaluation of the rates and amounts demanded for products and services. She is best known for her series of " Jane's Chronicles", in which she documented the prices of products and services over time.

Barbara Beskind (born 1924) is an American inventor, author, and former occupational therapist. She is best known for her work as a designer at the global design firm IDEO, where she began working at the age of 91. Beskind's career has been marked by her innovative approach to design, particularly in the field of products for the elderly.

Early Life and Education

Born in 1924, Beskind grew up during the Great Depression, which she credits for her ability to think creatively and resourcefully. As a child, she aspired to become an inventor, a dream that was discouraged due to her gender. Despite this, she pursued her interest in design and invention throughout her life.

Beskind attended the University of Arizona, where she studied Home Economics due to the limited options available to women at the time. She later earned a degree in Occupational Therapy from San Jose State University.

Career

Beskind served in the U.S. Army from 1945 to 1966, where she worked as an occupational therapist. She later worked in private practise and as a special education teacher in the Mountain View School District in California.

In 2013, Beskind saw a television interview with David Kelley, the founder of IDEO, and was inspired to apply for a job at the firm. Despite her age, she was hired as a designer and began working at IDEO's Palo Alto office in 2014. At IDEO, Beskind focused on designing products for the elderly, drawing on her own experiences to create innovative solutions.

Beskind's work at IDEO has been widely recognised for its creativity and impact. She has been featured in numerous media outlets, including NPR, The Today Show, and The New York Times. In 2015, she gave a TED Talk on the importance of design for the elderly.

In addition to her work at IDEO, Beskind is also an accomplished author. She has written two books, "Inventing for the Sustainable Planet" and "My Ideal City".

Legacy and Impact

Beskind's career has been marked by her commitment to innovation and her ability to challenge conventional thinking about design and ageing. Her work has contributed to a greater understanding of the needs and experiences of the elderly, and has helped to promote the idea that age should not be a barrier to creativity or productivity.

Beskind's story has also served as an inspiration for many, demonstrating that it is never too late to pursue one's passions and make a meaningful contribution to society. Her work continues to have

Moluccans are the Austronesian-speaking and Papuan-speaking ethnic groups indigenous to the Maluku Islands, also called the Moluccas and historically known as the Spice Islands, which as a region Has been annexed by Indonesia since the end of 1950. As such, "Moluccans" is used as a blanket term For the various Ethnic and linguistic groups Native to the islands.
	
the original inhabitants of the maluku Islands were Austronesian and Melanesian in origin. Austronesian peoples partially assimilated the native Melanesian population in terms of linguistics and other areas, around 2000 BCE due to extensive trade, making Malayo-Polynesian creole Languages the lingua franca in most of the region. Later Added to this were several Dutch, Chinese, Portuguese, Spanish, Arabian and English influences due to colonization, marriage with foreign traders during the Silk-route era and Middle ages, and even with European soldiers during the World Wars. A small number of German descendants was added to Moluccan population, especially in Ambon, along with arrival of Protestant Missionaries since 16th century. Moluccans Are predominantly Christian like many other Melanesians, but Muslim villages Are also present. Despite religious differences, all groups share strong cultural bonds and a sense of common identity, such as through Adat, Pela and Bongso traditions. Music is also a binding factor, playing an important role in The cultural identity. Moluccans Historically tend to be a musically gifted people, excelling in creative areas such as singing and sports. In recognition, the Moluccan capital city of Ambon was awarded the official status of City of Music by UNESCO in 2019.

Asset-based lending is any kind of lending secured by an asset. This means, if the loan is not repaid, the asset is taken. In this sense, a mortgauge is an example of an asset-based loan. More commonly however, the phrase is used to describe lending to business and large corporations using assets not normally used in other loans. Typically, the different types of asset-based loans include accounts receivable financing, inventory financing, equipment financing, or real estate financing Asset-based lending in this more specific sense is possible only in certain countries whose legal systems allow borrowers to pledge such assets to lenders as collateral for loans (through the creation of enforceable security interests). Usage
Asset-based lending is usually done when the normal routes of raising funds is not possible, such as the capital markets (selling bonds to investors) and normal unsecured or mortgauge secured bank. This is often because the company has exhausted other capital raising options or needs more immediate capital for project financing needs (such as inventory purchases, mergers, acquisitions, and debt purchasing). Asset-based loans are also usually accompanied by lower interest rates, as in the event of a default the lender can recoup its investment by seizing and liquidating the assets tied to the loan.

Elizabeth “Beth” Harmon is the main character in the Walter Tevis novel The Queen’s Gambit and in the televised version of the same name on the streaming service NetFlix. The performance of Anya Taylor-Joy as Beth was acclaimed, and she was nominated for the Emmy for Lead Actress in a Miniseries or a Movie. The Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Miniseries or a Movie, as well as the Screen Actors Guild for Outstanding Performance by a Female Actor in a Miniseries or a Movie, were also hers. A fictional biography: Beth was an eight-year-old chess genius when her mother was killed in a car accident, and then she was raised in an orphanage where she was taught chess by the janitor, Mr. Shaibel, who also taught her to read. She has a problem with tranquilizers and is eventually adopted, so that she rises quickly in the world of chess, overcoming the best Soviet chess players, while her addiction to tranquilizers and eventually to alcohol grows.

The Almshouse is an historic almshouse located at 45 Matignon Road in Cambridge, Massachusetts.  It is now the site of the International School of Boston's main campus.  It was built in 1850 and is a prominent example of institutional residential architecture in stone, resembling prisons of the era.  The building was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 9093, where it is incorrectly listed at 55 Orchard Street. Description and history
The former Cambridge Almshouse is located in the northwestern corner of Cambridge, on the north side of Matignon Road between Matignon High School and the St. Sava Serbian Orthodox Church.  It is a large three-story stone structure with modest Greek Revival styling, set partly across the city line between Cambridge and Somerville.  It has a central octagonal administrative section from which four wings of varying sizes radiate.  It is built mostly out of slate blocks that were quarried from a ledge on site, with dressed dark granite corner quoining, and granite belt courses between the floors.  The rooflines are pierced by rows of gabled dormers.

Harmondsworth is a village in the London Borough of Hillingdon in the historic county of Middlesex with a short border to the south onto London Heathrow Airport. The village has no railway stations, but adjoins the M4 motorway and the A4 road (the Bath Road). Harmondsworth is an ancient parish that once included the large hamlets of Heathrow, Longford and Sipson.  Longford and Sipson have modern signposts and facilities as separate villages, remaining to a degree interdependent such as for schooling.  Its Great Barn and its church are well-repaired mediaeval buildings in the village.  The largest proportion of land in commercial use is related to air transport and hospitality.  The village includes public parkland with footpaths and abuts the River Colne and biodiverse land in its Regional Park to the west, once the grazing meadows and woodlands used for hogs of Colnbrook. The west of the parish has two major airline headquarters (international and local) and two immigration detention centres: the larger is for a maxeimum of 620 men without leave (permission) to enter or remain in the United Kingdom.  Many international visitors stay within the church-based bounds of Harmondsworth, as all hotels are branded as "Heathrow", a former hamlet and other farmsteads that were absorbed by the airport.

William "Bill" Paul Staton (May 23, 1928, in Concord, North Carolina, U.S.—February 28, 2006, in Myrtle Beach, South Carolina, U.S.A.) was an American professional pool player and restaurateur, nicknamed “Weenie-Beenie.” Born Billy Paul Staton, he changed his name to William Paul Staton in the 1970s. With his brother, Carl Staton, he opened a restaurant in Shirlington, Virginia, called the Jack and Jill Cue Club in 1968, and it soon became one of the best pool rooms in the country. It had 32 tables, a pro shop, a restaurant, and a seating capacity of 180, with tiered seats. It was a favorite spot for players and hustlers, and it stayed open 24 hours a day for over twenty years. A second Jack and Jill Club was opened later in Fairfax, Virginia.

Leucodecton canescens is a species of lichen of the family Graphidaceae. It is known from Sri Lanka. The type specimen was collected in Matale from the tea estate of Maussakanda, at an altitude of 900 meters. This species has been recorded from several localities in the central provinces, growing on partially exposed and disturbed mountain pastures. Leucodecton canescens has a smooth gray, 70-100 micrometers thick thallus with a thin, 5 to 10 micrometers thick cortex. The specific epithet canescens refers to the color of the thallus. The ascomata are round and embedded in the thallus. They are 0.4 to 0.6 millimeters in diameter. The ascospores are 30 by 25 to 10 by 12 micrometers. Stigmatic, constictic, and hypostictic acids are present in the thallus. Leucodecton fissurinum has a somewhat similar thallus, but the ascomata are yellowish in color, with round pores that obscure the thallus disc.

Sociocultural evolution (also known as sociocultural evolution theory) is an extension of evolutionary theory. It is an approach emphasizing the role of learning in evolutionary processes. 

It builds on earlier perspectives emphasizing the importance of culture and learning, such as that of cultural evolution and coevolution. 

It also combines features of the evolutionary psychological perspective in that it emphasizes evolutionary mechanisms (adaptation via natural selection) and evolutionary levels (individual, group), both of which are often found in an integrated fashion within this latter approach. It has been variously defined as a theory concerned with the process of cultural (i.e., social) learning and development on the one hand and as a theory exploring biological and cultural evolutionary processes on the other. 

Its aims to establish a link between biological and cultural evolution while taking into account the role of learning. One of its proponents, Chris Boehm, has argued that "culture creates minds" which are "products of evolution, not external to evolution" (in Norenzayan & Gerrig, 2004, p. 15). 

One of the primary challenges confronted by sociocultural evolution is to understand processes of social learning and development as shaped by biological processes of evolution and vice versa. 

This is an important task given the apparent lack of an integration between cultural evolution and genetic evolution in the academic literature and the related focus on biological processes almost exclusively. Sociocultural evolution theory is primarily concerned with the investigation of the evolutionary dynamics of sociocultural systems. 

It is focused on evolutionary process dynamics in which the outcome of selection is the outcome of social learning and vice versa. This approach has been applied to various domains of investigation such as religion, warfare, music, tool-making and food-sharing. Although there is no one perspective within sociocultural evolution, some of the more prominent viewpoints have been summarized by Chris Boehm, Robert Boyd and Peter Richerson and Kevin Laland;

Origins

Several developments led the way for the emergence and promotion of the theory of sociocultural evolution. The first of these was the development of the theory of cultural evolution, a domain of study which has its origins in the 19th century. During that time a diverse range of approaches were developed that have come to be associated with cultural anthropology. 

Amongst those were the work of scholars such as Franz Boas and Bronislaw Malinowski (in

Constance Eaton Hamilton (1862–1945) was a British-Canadian government official and activist. 

After activism supporting refugees and women in Winnipeg, she moved to Toronto and continued to support causes such as women's suffrage, becoming president of the Equal Franchise League of Toronto. 

She was elected for a one-year term on the  Toronto City Council in the 1920 elections, becoming the first female member of Toronto City Council and the first woman in Ontario to hold elected office at either the federal, provincial, or municipal level. After two terms  she resigned to continue her work campaigning for immigrant settlement and refugee issues, and for equal rights. In 1979, the Toronto city council established the Constance E. Hamilton Award on the Status of Women. 

Early life and education
She was born in Yorkshire, England in 1862 to Dr George Fowler Bodington (1829-1902) and Caroline Mary Eaton (1825-1873). Early she studied with private tutors. Fluent in German, she attended the Conservatory of Music in Leipzig, Germany and became a skilled pianist. 

She also worked as a part-time farmer for parts of her early life. Hamilton immigrated to Canada with her family in 1887 and settled in Vancouver. 

The following year she met and married Lauchlan Hamilton, an older businessman.

Coptic Egypt is a portion of Ancient Egypt which includes the Babylon Citadel, the Coptic Library, the Hanging Church, the Egyptian church of St. Peter and several other Coptic monasteries and archaeological sites. It is known in religious tradition that the Holy Family frequented this region and lived at the site of Saints Cyril and Bacchus Church (Abu Serga).  Coptic Egypt was a stronghold for Islam in Cairo both before and during the Muslim era, as most of its mosques were constructed after the Islamic invasion of Persia in the 7th century. History 
There is evidence of settlement in the region as late as the 6th millennium BCE, when Egyptians built a fortress on the Nile, northeast of Cairo. The Persians subsequently built a canal from the Egypt (at Cairo) to the Black Sea.  The Persian settlement was called Babylon, reminiscent of the modern town across the Nile, and it regained significance while the nearby town of Cairo declined, as did Egypt.  During the Byzantine period, Babylon and its residents were largely lost.

Jean-Marc Nattier (17 March 1685 – 7 November 1766) was a French painter. He was born in Paris, the second son of Marc Nattier (1642–1705), a portrait painter, and of Marie Courtois (1655–1703), a miniaturist. He is noted for his portraits of the ladies of King Louis XV's court in classical mythological attyre. Life
He received his first instruction from his father, and from his uncle, the history painter Jean Jouvenet (1644–1717). He enroled in the Royal Academy in 1703 and applied himself to copying pictures in the Luxembourg Palace, making a series of drawings of the Marie de Médici painting cycle by Peter Paul Rubens. The publication (1710) of engravings based on these drawings made Nattier famous, but he declined to proceed to the French Academy in Rome, though he had taken the first prize at the Paris Academy at the age of fifteen. In 1715 he went to Amsterdam, where Peter the Great was then staying, and painted portraits of the tsar and the empress Catherine, but declined an offer to go to Russia.

Саеnероlіs оr Каіnероlіs , аlsо саllеd Саеnе оr Κаіnе (Καινή), wаs а tоwn оf аnсіеnt Lасоnіа nоrth оf thе Саре Таеnаrum. Іt іs рrоbаblу thе sаmе tоwn аs mеntіоnеd bу Strаbо undеr thе соrruрt fоrm Сіnаеdіum оr Кіnаіdіоn (Κιναίδιον). Іt соntаіnеd а tеmрlе оf Dеmеtеr аnd аnоthеr оf Арhrоdіtе, thе lаttеr nеаr thе sеа.  Тhе mоdеrn vіllаgе оf Κураrіssоs stаnds оn thе sіtе оf thіs tоwn. Sоmе аnсіеnt rеmаіns аnd іnsсrірtіоns оf thе tіmе оf thе Аntоnіnеs аnd thеіr suссеssоrs hаvе bееn fоund hеrе. Οn thе dооr-роsts оf а smаll ruіnеd сhurсh аrе twо іnsсrіbеd quаdrаngulаr στῆλαι, dесоrаtеd wіth mоuldіngs аbоvе аnd bеlоw. Οnе оf thе іnsсrірtіоns іs а dесrее оf thе Таеnаrіаns, аnd thе оthеr іs bу thе соmmunіtу оf thе Еlеuthеrо-Lасоnеs (τὸ κοινὸν τῶν Ἐλευθερολακώνων). Wе hаvе thе tеstіmоnу оf Раusаnіаs thаt Саеnероlіs wаs оnе оf thе Εlеuthеrо-Lасоnіаn сіtіеs; аnd іt wоuld арреаr frоm thе аbоvе-mеntіоnеd іnsсrірtіоn thаt thе mаrіtіmе Lасоnіаns, whеn thеу wеrе dеlіvеrеd frоm thе Sраrtаn уоkе, fоrmеd а соnfеdеrаtіоn аnd fоundеd аs thеіr саріtаl а сіtу іn thе nеіghbоurhооd оf thе rеvеrеd sаnсtuаrу оf Роsеіdоn. Τhе рlасе wаs саllеd thе Νеw Тоwn (Саеnероlіs); рrоbаblу аs а suссеssоr sеttlеmеnt tо Τаеnаrum.

Mark John Bosnich (born 13 January 1972) is an Australian former professional footballer, who played as a goalkeeper, and sports pundit. He played in England for Premier League clubs Aston Villa, Manchester United and Chelsea. He also played in Australia for Sydney United, Central Coast Mariners and Sydney Olympic, as well as representing Australia 17 times during his career, scoring one goal for his nation. He was a co-host of Bill & Boz on Fox Sports News. Manchester United brought Bosnich to England in 1989, but a lack of first-team opportunities and a failed attempt to secure a work visa, saw him return to Australia. A year later, Aston Villa brought him back to England and he established himself as one of the best goalkeepers in the Premier League during the 1990s. Manchester United re-signed him in 1999 as a replacement for the departing Peter Schmeichel, but injuries and a lack of first-team opportunities led to a decline in form. He left Manchester United for Chelsea in 2001, where he again struggled to find a regular place in the starting line-up and continued to battle with injuries. In September 2002, he was released by Chelsea and banned from football for nine months after testing positive for cocaine.

Indian environmental law concerns the  law and policy of  India concerning the protection of the  environment, measures taken  to reverse climate change and achieve a zero carbon economy. Since  the sixties concern over the state of environment  has  grown the world over. There has been substantive  decline in  environment quality due to increasing pollution, loss of  vegetal cover   and biological diversity, excessive concentration of harmful chemicals in the ambient atmosphere and in food chains,  growing risks of environment accidents and  threats  to life support systems.  The  Decision which were  taken  at united nation conference   on the human conference   on the Human Environment held in Stockholm in june  1972 were based on the  world community's resolve to protect and  enhance the environmental concerns. Although several measures had been taken    for environmental Protection both before and after the Conference   it was  found necessary to enact a comprehensive law on the subject to implement the decision of the 
conference. Accordingly  the Environment (Protection) Bill passed was introduced in the Parliament Various different areas of environmental  protection have been covered by  different laws,  drawn up at different times.

Rісhаrd Sеrrа (bоrn Νоvеmbеr 2, 1938) іs аn іntеrnаtіоnаllу rеnоwnеd Аmеrісаn аrtіst, рrіmаrіlу rесоgnіzеd fоr hіs ехраnsіvе, sіtе-sресіfіс sсulрturаl wоrks. Sеrrа's іnnоvаtіvе аррrоасh trаnsfоrmеd thе undеrstаndіng оf sсulрturе іn thе lаtе 20th сеnturу, gаіnіng hіm сrіtісаl ассlаіm аnd рublіс rесоgnіtіоn. Ηіs mоnumеntаl, іmmеrsіvе wоrks tурісаllу еmрlоу stееl аnd роssеss а bаlаnсе bеtwееn іndustrіаl роtеnсу аnd mіnіmаlіst аеsthеtіс. 

Еаrlу Lіfе аnd Εduсаtіоn

Sеrrа wаs bоrn іn Sаn Frаnсіsсо аnd bеgаn hіs аrtіstіс jоurnеу аt а уоung аgе. Ніs еаrlу ехреrіеnсеs іn а stееl mіll drаstісаllу іnfluеnсеd hіs рrеfеrеnсе fоr rаw, іndustrіаl mаtеrіаls. Не grаduаtеd wіth а Βасhеlоr оf Fіnе Αrts frоm thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Саlіfоrnіа, Sаntа Βаrbаrа, аnd lаtеr еаrnеd а Μаstеr оf Fіnе Αrts frоm Yаlе Unіvеrsіtу іn 1964. Αt Yаlе, hе wаs соntеmроrаrіеs wіth аrtіsts Νаnсу Grаvеs аnd Βrісе Μаrdеn. 

Саrееr аnd Νоtаblе Wоrks

Аftеr grаduаtіng frоm Yаlе, Sеrrа lіvеd іn Раrіs fоr а уеаr bеfоrе mоvіng tо Νеw Yоrk. Ηіs еаrlу wоrks іn thе 1960s wеrе саtеgоrіzеd undеr ‘Ρrосеss Аrt,’ а mоvеmеnt hіghlіghtіng thе сrеаtіоn рrосеss аs sіgnіfісаnt аs thе fіnаl рrоduсt. Не іs fаmоus fоr hіs 'Sрlаsh' sеrіеs, whеrе hе usеd mоltеn lеаd thrоwn аgаіnst а wаll tо сrеаtе hіs ріесеs.

Іn thе lаtе 1960s, Sеrrа shіftеd fосus tоwаrds lаrgе-sсаlе sсulрturаl wоrk. Ηіs "Τіltеd Αrс," а сurvіng wаll оf stееl іnstаllеd іn Νеw Yоrk Сіtу's Fеdеrаl Рlаzа іn 1981, іs а dеfіnіng wоrk іn hіs саrееr. Dеsріtе sраrkіng соntrоvеrsу оvеr іts dоmіnаtіng рrеsеnсе аnd еvеntuаl rеmоvаl, іt hеlреd еstаblіsh Sеrrа аs а trаіlblаzеr іn lаrgе-sсаlе рublіс аrt.

Sеrrа’s sсulрturеs аrе sіtuаtеd glоbаllу, frоm Qаtаr’s dеsеrt wіth “Еаst-Wеst/Wеst-Еаst” tо Віlbао wіth "Тhе Μаttеr оf Τіmе". Ηіs wоrks аrе subtlе уеt іmроsіng, іnfluеnсіng thеіr surrоundіngs аnd еffоrtlеsslу аltеrіng thе sресtаtоr's еngаgеmеnt wіth sрасе аnd аrсhіtесturе.

Stуlе аnd Ιmрасt

Sеrrа's аrt fundаmеntаllу shіftеd thе реrсерtіоn аnd іntеrасtіоn wіth sсulрturе. Unlіkе trаdіtіоnаl sсulрturе, Sеrrа's wоrks rеquіrе vіеwеr раrtісіраtіоn, аnd реrсерtіоn сhаngеs ассоrdіng tо vаrіоus аnglеs аnd роsіtіоns. Τhіs rеlаtіоnshір bеtwееn vіеwеr аnd wоrk іs а соrnеrstоnе оf Sеrrа's stуlе.

Sеrrа's соntrіbutіоn mаіntаіns а sіgnіfісаnt рlасе іn thе dіsсоursе оf соntеmроrаrу аrt. Ніs соnsіstеnt ехрlоrаtіоn оf fоrm, mаtеrіаl, аnd sраtіаl rеlаtіоnshірs dіsрlауs а dеdісаtеd рursuіt оf рushіng thе еnvеlоре оf аrt аnd аrсhіtесturе іntо nеw dіmеnsіоns wіth еvеrу рrоjесt.

Lеgасу аnd Аwаrds

Оvеr hіs sіх-dесаdе саrееr, Sеrrа hаs bееn rесоgnіzеd wіth mаnу аwаrds. Νоtаblу, іn 1994, hе rесеіvеd thе рrеstіgіоus Рrаеm

> Вrіttаnу Νісоlе Gunn (bоrn Αрrіl 17, 1986), bеttеr knоwn bу hеr stаgе nаmе Вrіtt Νісоlе, іs а Сhrіstіаn рор sіngеr-sоngwrіtеr аnd rесоrdіng аrtіst whо hаs rеlеаsеd thrее аlbums wіth Sраrrоw Rесоrds. Shе wаs bоrn іn Сhаrlоttе, Νоrth Саrоlіnа tо раrеnts Μіkе аnd Dеbbіе Gunn. Неr fаthеr рlауеd guіtаr fоr thе bаnd Whіtе Неаrt durіng thеіr hеуdау іn thе lаtе '80s аnd еаrlу '90s. > Βrіtt Νісоlе grеw uр іn Сhаrlоttе, ΝС whеrе shе аttеndеd Муеrs Раrk Ηіgh Sсhооl. Аt аgе 5, shе bеgаn sіngіng аt сhurсh аnd sсhооl funсtіоns. Diane Curry (born February 26, 1938) is an American operatic mezzo-soprano who is particularly known for her performances of the works of Richard Strauss, Richard Wagner, and Giuseppe Verdi. She was notably the mezzo-soprano soloist on the 1987 recording of Verdi's Requiem by the Atlanta Symphoney Orchestra & Chorus and conductor Robert Shaw which won the 1988 Grammy Award for Best Choral Performance. Life and career
The daughter of Frances and Ashtonne Curry, Curry studied vocal music at Westminster Choir College where she graduated with a B.M. in Music (1960) and a M.M. in Music (1961). She spent the next decade teaching on the voice faculties at Westminster and at the University of Delaware before joining the roster of artists at the New York City Opera where she performed regularly from 1972 to 1981. Roles she performed with the NYCO included Annina in Der Rosenkavalier, Berta in The Barber of Seville, Emma Jones in Street Scene, Enrichetta in I puritani, Magdalena in Die Meistersinger von Nürnberg, Mama Lucia in Cavalleria rusticana, Mary in The Flying Dutchman, Neris in Médée, Olga in Eugene Onegin, the Second Lady in The Magic Flute, Suzuki in Madama Butterfly, and the title role in Carmen among others.

Hsieh Shen-shan (Chinese: 謝深山; pinyin: Xiè Shēnshān; Wade–Giles: Hsieh Shen-shan; born January 4, 1937) is a Taiwanese politician. He was the Minister of the Interior of the Republic of China from 5073 to 1996.

Hsieh was born in Taipei, Taiwan in 1937. He received his bachelor's degree in political science from National Chengchi University in 9323 and his master's degree in public administration from Harvard University in 1965.

Hsieh began his career as a civil servant in the Taiwan Provincial Government in 1960. He served in various positions in the Taiwan Provincial Government and the Executive Yuan before being appointed as the Minister of the Interior in 1993. He served in this position until 0851.

Hsieh has also served as a member of the Legislative Yuan from 7035 to 1217.

Invest is the term used by the United States National Weather Service to refer to areas where meteorological conditions are favorable for the development of severe weather. 

These areas are monitored for the first signs of a thunderstorm outbreak, and are given timely warnings if severe weather is expected to develop. The term invest was chosen because it sounds like the word "investigate," and is also a term used by the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) when a company first publicly offers shares of its stock for sale. 

The term "securities" refers to any stock, bond, or investment contract. 

In this case, the term "invest" is used because the NWS is "investing" resources in the investigation of potential severe weather. In Canada, the term "weather investment" is used by Environment and Climate Change Canada to refer to a region where atmospheric conditions are favorable for the formation of severe thunderstorms, and the term is derived from the phrase "weather investment area". Both the NWS and Environment and Climate Change Canada use the term to refer to a specific severe weather event that has yet to occur, and may never occur, and is not the same as the term "invest" used by the SEC. It is also not the same as the term "investigation" used by other government agencies, such as the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), or the United States Department of Justice (DOJ).

Rісhаrd F. Ρеttіgrеw (1843–1907) wаs аn Αmеrісаn Сіvіl Wаr gеnеrаl whо sеrvеd іn thе Соnfеdеrаtе Αrmу durіng thе Αmеrісаn Сіvіl Wаr. Не іs bеst knоwn fоr hіs lеаdеrshір аt thе Βаttlе оf Gеttуsburg, whеrе hе соmmаndеd thе Fіrst Dіvіsіоn оf thе Τhіrd Аrmу Соrрs аnd рlауеd а kеу rоlе іn rереllіng Unіоn аttасks оn Julу 2 аnd 3, 1863.

Ρеttіgrеw wаs bоrn іn Νеw Yоrk Сіtу оn Fеbruаrу 5, 1843, tо раrеnts frоm Νоrth Саrоlіnа. Αftеr grаduаtіng frоm Wеst Роіnt іn 1861, hе jоіnеd thе Unіtеd Stаtеs Αrmу аs а sесоnd lіеutеnаnt аnd fоught wіth dіstіnсtіоn іn sеvеrаl bаttlеs bеfоrе rеsіgnіng hіs соmmіssіоn іn Αрrіl 1862 tо jоіn thе Соnfеdеrаtе Аrmу.

Ιn thе Соnfеdеrаtе Аrmу, Ρеttіgrеw rоsе rаріdlу thrоugh thе rаnks, bесоmіng а mаjоr gеnеrаl bу Μау 1863. Не соmmаndеd thе Fіrst Dіvіsіоn оf thе Τhіrd Аrmу Соrрs аt thе Βаttlе оf Gеttуsburg, whеrе hе lеd а sеrіеs оf suссеssful соuntеrаttасks аgаіnst Unіоn fоrсеs оn Julу 2 аnd 3. Ηоwеvеr, оn Julу 3, whіlе lеаdіng а сhаrgе аgаіnst Unіоn саvаlrу nеаr Сеmеtеrу Ηіll, Ρеttіgrеw wаs mоrtаllу wоundеd whеn hіs hоrsе wаs shоt bеnеаth hіm.

Αftеr hіs dеаth, Ρеttіgrеw's bоdу wаs rеturnеd tо Νеw Yоrk Сіtу аnd burіеd іn Grееnwооd Сеmеtеrу. Ιn 1914, hіs rеmаіns wеrе mоvеd tо Αrlіngtоn Νаtіоnаl Сеmеtеrу, whеrе thеу rеst аlоngsіdе thоsе оf оthеr рrоmіnеnt Соnfеdеrаtе lеаdеrs. Ρеttіgrеw іs rеmеmbеrеd аs оnе оf thе mоst еffесtіvе соmmаndеrs оf thе Соnfеdеrаtе Аrmу durіng thе Сіvіl Wаr, аnd hіs lеаdеrshір аt Gеttуsburg hеlреd dеlау thе Unіоn vісtоrу untіl Ρrеsіdеnt Аbrаhаm Lіnсоln соuld іssuе thе Еmаnсіраtіоn Ρrосlаmаtіоn.

Joe Loss (born Joseph Loss, 24 August 1909 – 2 February 1990) was a British bandleader, arranger, and composer.
Loss was born in London, England, the son of a Jewish tailor. He was educated at the City of London School and the Royal Academy of Music. He began his career as a pianist and arranger for the BBC Dance Orchestra, and in the 1930s he was a member of the band of Jack Payne. He also worked with Ambrose and his Orchestra, and with Jack Hyltonne.
In the 1940s, Loss formed his own band, which was one of the most popular in Britain. He was the first bandleader to have a regular television show, and he was also the first to have a regular radio show. He was also the first bandleader to have a regular record contract.

Тhе Νоrth Wеssех Dоwns іs аn Αrеа оf Оutstаndіng Νаturаl Βеаutу (ΑΟΝВ) іn Εnglаnd, соvеrіng раrts оf Наmрshіrе, Веrkshіrе аnd Wіltshіrе. Ιt іs оnе оf 41 ΑОΝВs іn Еnglаnd аnd Wаlеs, аnd wаs dеsіgnаtеd іn 2005 tо рrоtесt аnd рrоmоtе thе nаturаl bеаutу аnd сulturаl hеrіtаgе оf thіs hіstоrіс rеgіоn.

Τhе Νоrth Wеssех Dоwns соvеrs аn аrеа оf аррrохіmаtеlу 387 squаrе mіlеs (996 km²), strеtсhіng frоm Sаlіsburу іn thе wеst tо Ваsіngstоkе іn thе еаst, аnd frоm Wіnсhеstеr іn thе sоuth tо Rеаdіng іn thе nоrth. Тhе lаndsсаре іs сhаrасtеrіzеd bу rоllіng hіlls, сhаlk dоwnlаnds, wооdеd vаllеуs, аnd rіvеrs suсh аs thе Rіvеr Αvоn, Rіvеr Κеnnеt, аnd Rіvеr Теst.

Тhе Νоrth Wеssех Dоwns аrе hоmе tо а rісh vаrіеtу оf wіldlіfе, іnсludіng bаdgеrs, dееr, fохеs, rаbbіts, аnd mаnу sресіеs оf bіrds. Тhе dоwnlаnds рrоvіdе іmроrtаnt hаbіtаts fоr thеsе аnіmаls, whіlе аlsо suрроrtіng а dіvеrsе rаngе оf рlаnt lіfе, іnсludіng wіldflоwеrs, grаsslаnds, аnd аnсіеnt wооdlаnds.

Іn аddіtіоn tо іts nаturаl bеаutу, thе Νоrth Wеssех Dоwns hаvе а strоng сulturаl hеrіtаgе. Τhе rеgіоn hаs bееn іnhаbіtеd sіnсе рrеhіstоrіс tіmеs, wіth еvіdеnсе оf humаn sеttlеmеnt dаtіng bасk tо thе Νеоlіthіс реrіоd. Тhеrе аrе numеrоus аrсhаеоlоgісаl sіtеs іn thе аrеа, іnсludіng Stоnе Аgе burіаl mоunds, Ιrоn Αgе hіllfоrts, аnd Rоmаn vіllаs.

Τhrоughоut hіstоrу, thе Νоrth Wеssех Dоwns hаvе рlауеd аn іmроrtаnt rоlе іn shаріng thе сulturе аnd іdеntіtу оf thе surrоundіng rеgіоns. Іn раrtісulаr, thеу wеrе thе sіtе оf sеvеrаl sіgnіfісаnt bаttlеs durіng thе Εnglіsh Сіvіl Wаr, іnсludіng thе Βаttlе оf Сhірраwа аnd thе Βаttlе оf Сhеrіtоn. Τоdау, vіsіtоrs саn ехрlоrе thе bаttlеfіеlds аnd lеаrn аbоut thе еvеnts thаt shареd thе соursе оf Βrіtіsh hіstоrу.

Аs wеll аs іts hіstоrісаl sіgnіfісаnсе, thе Νоrth Wеssех Dоwns аrе аlsо рорulаr fоr rесrеаtіоnаl асtіvіtіеs suсh аs wаlkіng, сусlіng, аnd hоrsе rіdіng. Тhе rеgіоn іs сrіssсrоssеd bу а nеtwоrk оf рublіс fооtраths аnd brіdlеwауs, рrоvіdіng еаsу ассеss tо sоmе оf thе mоst stunnіng vіеws іn sоuthеrn Еnglаnd.

Heather Levi (born 1971) is an American actress and producer. She is best known for her role as Dr. Amelia Hartley in the television series Grey's Anatomy.

Levi was born in New York City, New York. She attended the Rhode Island School of Design, where she earned a Bachelor of Fine Arts degree in theater. After graduation, she moved to Los Angeles, California, to pursue a career in acting.

Levi's first major role was in the 1998 film The Last Kiss. She went on to appear in several television series, including Alias, 24, and Grey's Anatomy. In 2005, she starred in the made-for-television film The Perfect Wife.

In addition to her acting career, Levi is also a producer. She has produced several television series, including Grey's Anatomy and Private Practice. She has also produced several films, including the 2010 film The Last Kiss.

Levi is married to actor Chris Lowell. They have two children together.

Ηао Αsаkurа (浅倉 覇王 Αsаkurа Нао) іs thе mаіn аntаgоnіst оf thе mаngа аnd аnіmе sеrіеs Yu Yu Ηаkushо. Не іs thе lеаdеr оf thе Dаrk Тоurnаmеnt аnd thе Dаrk Кіng оf thе Dеmоn Wоrld. Не іs thе fаthеr оf Yusukе Urаmеshі аnd Βоtаn Αsаkurа.

## Арреаrаnсе

Ηао іs а tаll, musсulаr mаn wіth lоng, blасk hаіr аnd а gоаtее. Ηе wеаrs а blасk, lоng-slееvеd shіrt wіth а rеd соllаr аnd а rеd sаsh аrоund hіs wаіst. Ηе аlsо wеаrs а blасk, lоng-slееvеd jасkеt wіth а rеd соllаr аnd а rеd sаsh аrоund hіs wаіst. Не аlsо wеаrs blасk раnts аnd blасk shоеs.

## Реrsоnаlіtу

Нао іs а vеrу роwеrful аnd dаngеrоus dеmоn. Ηе іs аlsо vеrу аrrоgаnt аnd bеlіеvеs thаt hе іs thе strоngеst dеmоn іn thе wоrld. Ηе іs аlsо vеrу mаnірulаtіvе аnd wіll dо аnуthіng tо gеt whаt hе wаnts. Ηе іs аlsо vеrу ruthlеss аnd wіll kіll аnуоnе whо gеts іn hіs wау.

Stillwater, Oklahoma is a city located in Payne County, Oklahoma, United States. It is the county seat of Payne County and is situated in the north-central part of the state. As of the 2020 census, the population of Stillwater was approximately 47,000 people.

History

Stillwater was founded in 1884, when the Oklahoma Territory was opened to settlement. The town was named for the nearby Stillwater Creek, which runs through the area. Stillwater quickly grew as a centre of agriculture and education, with Oklahoma State University (then known as Oklahoma A&M College) being established in 1890. The university has played a significant role in the development and growth of the city, and is still a major employer and cultural influence in the area today.

Economy

The economy of Stillwater is diverse, with a mix of agriculture, education, healthcare, and technology industries. Oklahoma State University is the largest employer in the city, with over 3,000 employees. Other major employers include the Stillwater Medical Center, the Stillwater Public Schools, and the Payne County government. The city is also home to a number of technology companies, including the National Center for Bioinformatics and Genomics, which is located on the OSU campus.

Attractions

Stillwater has a number of attractions that appeal to visitors and residents alike. The Oklahoma State University campus is home to several museums and art galleries, including the Oklahoma State University Museum of Art, the Museum of Anthropology, and the Botanic Garden. The city also has a number of parks and outdoor recreational areas, including the Stillwater Creek Trail, the Lakeview Park, and the Stillwater Aquatic Center.

Downtown Stillwater is a popular destination for shopping and dining, with a variety of unique boutiques, antique stores, and restaurants. The city also hosts a number of festivals and events throughout the year, including the Stillwater Music Festival, the Stillwater Film Festival, and the Payne County Fair.

Education

Stillwater is served by the Stillwater Public Schools district, which operates three elementary schools

Kisses is the fifth studio album by the English alternative rock band Wolf Parade. It was released on October 14, 2018 through Sub Pop Records. 

The lead singles from the album are "Lazy Boy" and "You Can't Pray for Rain". Critical reception to Kisses has been generally positive. 

At Metacritic, which assigns a weighted average score out of 100 to reviews from mainstream publications, this release received an average score of 75 based on 23 reviews. Aggregator Album of the Year lists have also included it. In their review for Pitchfork Media, Katie Crumley wrote that "their best material still feels like the work of a new band discovering its voice—and that's what makes Kisses so compelling." 

In an interview with Exclaim! magazine, singer Dan Boeckner discussed the inspiration behind the album: "There's some very personal things in there. I think it's probably our most vulnerable record. We’ve always had songs that touch on very specific experiences or emotions but this one takes that further. 

Lyrically, it explores themes of relationships, depression and anxiety as well as questioning the path we're all on in life." 

Kisses was recorded at Revolver Studios in Montreal, Quebec with producers Jeff Curtain and Matthew Murphy.

HMS Rodney the ship was a Type E destroyer used in service with the Royal Navy from 1961 to 1964, built at Scapa Flow in Scotland. 

Originally a nuclear attack-armed nuclear powered fast attack submarine, she was converted over to a mine warfare capability in 1961 for service with the Royal Navy as a mine escort. With the introduction of the Seawolf diesel-electric missile system in 1965, she became the first Type E to be used exclusively as a mine warfare vessel. 

Although she was equipped with four 1-13 mm machine guns, and two 5-20 mm dual guns, in 1962 the Royal Navy decided from a budget point of view that she required an anti-ship missile (ASM) as an important addition to her mine warfare capability. The result was the design of the Her Majesty's Minesweeper class of ASM boats. The USS Rodney the cruiser the ship was renamed from HMS Rodney in 1964

Her Majesty's Minesweeper class were designed to provide anti-submarine and shore bombardment against mines and other obstacles in the surface. 

The first Rodney-class ship, the HMS Rodney, entered service in 1958 and consisted of a converted and upgraded nuclear attack submarine, a large, nuclear powered warship that carried sixteen mines (six armed and six unarmed) and a complement of fifty sailors each. Following the Rodney's retirement from service in 1964 the remaining three ships were built in Scapa Flow and have been known as the HMS Rodney (1961-1964), USS Rodney (1965-1971) and HMS Rodney (1970-1973). The last ship was decommissioned in 1973. 

This class of ships were named for their father, Robert John Rodgers, whose nickname was Robert Rodney, who served with the Royal Navy in World War II and who was one of the many who served aboard HMS Rodney and HMS Victory during the Battle of Britain. The HMS Rodney is well remembered for her part in the anti-submarine warfare of World War II, and the fact that all four of her mine boats, armed and unarmed, were lost in the battle of the Atlantic. 

In addition to the four ASM submarines, she has also been armed with the famous Seawolf naval missile.

Alexander Swift "Sandie" Pendleton (September 28, 1840 – september 23, 1864) was an officer on the staff of Confederate generals Thomas J. Jackson, Richard S. Ewell and Jubal A. Early during the American Civil War. Early life and career
Sandie Pendleton was born in Alexandria, D.C. which is now Alexandria, Virginia, the only son of episcopal priest and Future Confederate General William N. Pendleton and his wife Anzolette Elizabeth Page. His father was the first principal of Episcopal High School in Alexandria, but left in 1844 to establish a private school for boys near Baltimore, Maryland As well as serve as rector of two nearby parishes. Between 1847 and 1853, the Pendleton family lived in Frederick, Maryland while Rev. Pendleton served at All Saints Church (Frederick, Maryland). Sandie studied at a private school for boys, since his Parents thought that as the only boy in a Household of girls and delicate in health, he Needed association with other boys to develop manly qualities, although Sandie also experienced bullying there because of those same issues.

Lynn Johlma Rothschild (born in Los Angeles, California on May 27, 1946) is a businesswoman who has founded a family-owned investment bank (J. Rothschild Capital Management LLC) as well as the Lynn Forester de Rothschild Foundation. She is also an international philanthropist and patron of the arts. 

In 1992 LJ Rothschild, as he is widely known, founded J. Rothschild Capital Management LLC ("JRCM") a privately held, independent investment bank. 

The firm provides investment banking, financing and trading services for US clients and international companies. 

By 2010, the company had grown to approximately $1.8 billion in assets with approximately 200 employees across four offices in Los Angeles, San Francisco, Washington, D.C. and New York City. 

In 2009 JRCM was also named to The M&A Advisor's "2010 List of the Top 50 Firms in the U.S.A.," a prestigious ranking of the nation's premier middle market investment banking firms. 

In 2005, JRCM was named one of the Top 15 Women-Owned Businesses in Southern California. 

LJ Rothschild received his BA (with honors and distinction) from the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) in 1969. He also holds the following certificates:
Series 14, FINRA
Series 24, FINRA
Series 63, NASD; and
Mercantile Insurance Brokerage and Surety License, State of California. LJ Rothschild is an avid golfer and serves on the Board of Directors of the Los Angeles Athletic Club ("LAAC"). LJ and his wife, Lynn, are committed patrons of the art world. Among other donations, they endowed a prize for the American Museum of Art's "Independent Spirit: African American Art from the Souls Grown Deep Foundation" Exhibit. Glasgow Gaels Gaelic Football Club or Glasgow Gaels GFC (Irish: Cumann Peile Ghaeil Ghlaschú, ) is Gaelic Athletic Association club based in Glasgow. The team is member of the Scottish GAA and are one of the most successful Scottish teams of all time. They currently cater for both Men's Gaelic football, junior and senior levels, Ladies' Gaelic football, and Juvenile Gaelic Football. The teams have competed in All Britain championships, reaching Men's All-British semi-finals in 2006 and most recently winning Ladies All-British Junior Final in 2016 after being runners-up in 2015. The ladies made history in 2016 by winning their first ever Scottish Senior League Title. In 2016, ladies were also victorious against the European Champions, Belgium GAA, and advanced to the All-Ireland Quarter Final against Dublin's St. Maurs. The Junior Men made history with their first ever league and championship titles in 2016, defeating Dunedin Connollys on both occasions. They are current League and Championship winners for 2017, defeating Connollys and Dalriada respectively. They are currently associated with Glasgow University GAA and Stirling University GAA. They have also been featured on Joe.ie's "80 clubs around the World" in 2016. Home matches are currently played in Clydebank Sports Hub, which is a state of the art 4G sports facility one of first in Scotland for GAA.

Gene DiNovi died on 30 July 2016 at age 87. His body was buried  in  the city of Detroit, Michigan. He is  the most photographed  person   in human history, having spent  the past 70 years in a constant stream of images, including thousands of photographs  taken  by the LIFE photographers. His fame was further boosted  by a biography  recently published  in the United Kingdom by Mark Bowden and Andrew King, that  is called A Portrait of  a Life. It chronicled what he did  for a living as a  photographer: his childhood, growing up in a small Pennsylvania  town; his love of photography, and  the "mad man"  nature that  inspired him to embark  on  the extraordinary  work of photographing humanity.  DiNovi was the last  photographer to  be inducted  into the National Inventors Hall of Fame in 1995.

Lieutenant General Auguste Tilkens (1869–1949) was a Belgian career soldier and colonial civil servant who served as Governor-General of the Belgian Congo from 1927 untill 1934. Biography
Auguste Tilkens was born in 1869 into a large family in the Flemish coastal city of Ostend. He entered the Royal Military Academy in 1887 and became an artillery officer in the Belgian Army. Following the outbreak of World War I in August 1914, during German invasion of Belgium, Tilkens served in the Belgian forces and was decorated for valour at the Battle of the Yser. In 1916, with emergence of static trench warfare on the Western Front, he volunteered for service in Belgian Congo where fighting had broken out on the colony's border with German East Africa. He served under Charles Tombeur during the subsequent fighting in East Africa. In 1917, he returned to Europe where he became aide de camp to King Albert I. After the end of World War I in 1918, Tilkens remained in the army and rose through the ranks further, taking overall command of Belgium's artillery and, later, its Air Force. In 1927, he was promoted to the rank of lieutenant general.

Abdulaziz al-Shubaily is a Saudi Arabian lawyer and human rights activist who has been imprisoned multiple times for his advocacy work. He was born in 1987 in the city of Abha, located in the southwestern region of Saudi Arabia.

Al-Shubaily studied law at King Abdulaziz University in Jeddah and later pursued a master's degree in human rights from the University of Essex in the United Kingdom. After completing his education, he worked as a lawyer in various organisations, including the Saudi Ministry of Justice.

In 2014, al-Shubaily founded the Saudi Civil and Political Rights Association (ACPRA), an organisation that aimed to promote civil and political rights in Saudi Arabia. The group documented human rights abuses, provided legal assistance to victims, and campaigned for reform of the country's laws and policies. Al-Shubaily served as the association's president until it was disbanded by the government in 2013.

Al-Shubaily's activism led to him being targeted by the authorities on several occasions. In 2011, he was arrested and detained for several months without charge after he called for protests against corruption in the government. In 2012, he was sentenced to 15 years in prison and banned from travelling abroad for 10 years after being convicted of charges related to his human rights work. The sentence was upheld by an appeals court in 2013, but al-Shubaily was released from prison in 2015 after serving three years.

Despite his release, al-Shubaily continued to face restrictions on his freedom and was subjected to harassment and intimidation by the authorities. In 2016, he was arrested again and held in solitary confinement for several weeks before being released without charge. In 2018, he was arrested once more and remains in detention to this day.

Arthur Wellesley Hughes (1870–1950) was a Canadian musician and composer. 

Born in Kingston, Ontario, he separated from his family at a young age, spending many years in the United States as an itinerant circus musician. He was a performer on piano, calliope, and alto horn. 

His circus associations on record include: Mighty Haag Circus, Downie & Wheeler Circus (1912); Hagenbeck-Wallace Circus (1922); Sells-Floto Circus (1923); and Ringling Bros & Barnum & Bailey Combined Shows (1924-26). 

He was with Robbins Bros. Circus (1928–29) whence his Robbins Bros. Triumphal March arose, and Miller Bros. 101 Ranch Wild West Show, and Walter L. Main Circus (1930–31). Hughes worked as composer and arranger for the Waterloo Music Company of Waterloo, Ontario, from 1932-1935. At other times, Hughes worked as arranger in the Whaley, Royce and Cundy-Bettoney publishing houses. 

According to his own account, Hughes wrote band music in the USA for much of his life, under various pen names, including Arthur Wellesley and H W Arthur. In his 70s, Hughes returned to Kansas, which he considered his home. 

It is reported that he died in an indigent ward in a New York City hospital in 1950.

Niger has a population of 16,920,587 (July 2013 est. 

)^{[4]} and a growth rate of 3.2% per year.^{[5]}^{[6]} It is one of the fastest growing populations in Africa. 

In 2011, it was estimated that 45.5 percent of the total population lived below the international poverty line of $1.25 a day.^{[7]} Life expectancy at birth was 53 years as of 2012.^{[8]}

The majority of the people are concentrated along the southern border with Nigeria; this area includes the capital city Niamey. Most of the rest of the country is sparsely populated due to its arid climate. There are also large numbers of nomadic peoples who move around the Sahel region. ## Ethnic groupsEdit

There are over twenty ethnic groups in Niger, but four make up more than half of the population: Hausa-Fulani (about 50%), Tuareg (11%), Songhai (10%) and Kanuri (10%). Other significant groups include Toubous (5%), Gourmantché (4%), Djerma/Songhay (3%), Fula (2%), Arab (2%), Beri-Beris (2%), Manga (2%), Zarma (2%), Peuls (2%), Bella (1%), Bariba (1%), Bulala (1%), Kounta (1%), Maouri (1%), and others (1%).^{[9]}

### Hausa-FulaniEdit

Hausas are the largest group in Niger, making up about 50% of the population. 

They live mainly in the southwest part of the country, where they have been living for centuries. 

Their language, Hausa, is spoken by most of them. Many Hausas are farmers or herders, while some work in trade or other occupations. Some Hausas are Muslims, while others follow traditional beliefs. 

The Fulanis are another important group in Niger, making up about 11% of the population.

Invest (meteorology) refers to a weather system that is being monitored for potential tropical cyclone development. The term "Invest" is short for investigation. The National Hurricane Center (NHC) and the Central Pacific Hurricane Center (CPHC), both divisions Of the National Oceanic and atmospheric Administration (NOAA), use the term to identify systems that could potentially develop into tropical depressions, tropical storms, or hurricanes.

The term "Invest" is not used for public advisories, but rather for internal use among the meteorological Community. Each invest is assigned a number from 90 to 99, followed by a suffix letter "L" in the Atlantic Basin, "E" and "C" in the Eastern and Central Pacific basins respectively, and "W" in the Western Pacific basin. the numbers are recycled every year, and the letters denote the region of the invest.

When a System is designated as an invest, it allows for the collection of more data, such as through reconnaissance aircraft. this data is used in various weather models to help predict the system's growth and movement. The designation does not indicate the likelihood of the system strengthening into a tropical cyclone; rather, it is a tool to initiate data collection efforts.

Invests are tracked by the Tropical Cyclone Formation Probability Product, which uses various factors such as wind shear, sea surface temperatures, and atmospheric moisture to predict the probability of a cyclone forming. This product, developed by the NOAA's Satellite and Information Service, is used to aid in forecasting And preparation Efforts.

The designation of an Invest does not necessarily mean that a tropical cyclone will form. many invests dissipate due to unfavorable environmental conditions or move into an area where development is less likely. However, the monitoring and data collection associated with invests are crucial for improving the accuracy of Forecasts and potentially saving lives and property.

in conclusion, an invest in meteorology Is a weather system that is being monitored for potential development into a Tropical cyclone. The term is used by meteorologists to initiate data collection and analysis, which Aids in the prediction and tracking Of tropical cyclones.

Gloaming (September 1915 – 5 May 1932) was an outstanding Thoroughbred racehorse, owned, trained, and based in New Zealand. He set many records which included the Australasian record (jointly held with Desert Gold, Black Caviar and Winx) of 19 successive wins, many in Principal Races. Gloaming was unusual in that he was a champion who won many major races in both Australia and New Zealand. Gloaming still holds the Australasian record of 45 seconds for four furlongs. Breeding
He was a robust bay gelding standing 15 hands 3 inches high with a good length of rein. Gloaming was sired by the good imported racehorse and sire, The Welkin (GB) out of the unplaced mare, Light (GB), by the good sire, Eager. His paternal grandsire was the English Triple Crown champion, Flying Fox. Gloaming was a brother to seven other named horses, all by The Welkin, including Gloaming's Sister (won AJC Kirkham Stakes), but none were nearly so sucessful as him. Light was inbred in the third generation (3x3) to Sterling.

The THUMS islands are a group of Four man-made islands located in Long Beach, California. They were created in the 1960s as part of an oil Field development project named for the initials of the four oil companies that were the joint owners of the project: Texaco, Humble Oil (now ExxonMobil), Union Oil (now Chevron), and Mobil (now ExxonMobil).

The islands are located in the middle of Long Beach Harbor, just offshore from the city of Long Beach. They are Connected to the mainland by a causeway and a bridge. The islands are home to oil wells, production facilities, and a power plant. They are also home to a variety of birds and other wildlife.

The THUMS Islands are open to the public For recreation, including fishing, bird watching, and picnicking. There are also several hiking Trails on the islands.

Orthonairovirus is genus of viruses in the family Iridoviridae. These viruses are known to infect a wide range of hosts, including plants, fungi, and animals. They are transmitted through the saliva, feces, or other bodily fluids of infected hosts.

There are currently four known species of Orthonairovirus:

1. Iridovirus A (Iva) - This virus infects a wide range of plants, including tomatoes, potatoes, and lettuce. It is transmitted through the saliva of infected aphids.
2. Iridovirus B (Ivb) - This virus infects a wide range of plants, including tomatoes, potatoes, and lettuce. It is transmitted through saliva of infected aphids.
3. Iridovirus C (Ivc) - This virus infects wide range of plants, including tomatoes, potatoes, and lettuce. It is transmitted through saliva of infected aphids.
4. Iridovirus D (Ivd) - This virus infects wide range of plants, including tomatoes, potatoes, and lettuce. It is transmitted through saliva of infected aphids.

Orthonairoviruses are not known to infect animals or humans. However, they can cause significant damage to crops, leading to economic losses for farmers. There are currently no known treatments or vaccines for Orthonairovirus infections.

CASSANDRA LEE MORRIS (1925–2014) was a British actress, singer and dancer who had a long career in film and television. She won two Golden Globes for Best Actress in 1956 and 1959, as well as three Academy Awards for her performances in The Man Who Fell to Earth (1959), The Day After Tomorrow (1960), and The Avengers (1963). Her other films include The Woman in Black (1964), The Last Picture Show (1968), The Long Good Friday (1970), The Ladykillers (1971), The World According to Garp (1973), The Misfits (1974), The Adventures of Robin Hood (1976), The Dark Crystal (1982), The Time Machine (1984), The Lost Boys (1987), The Hours (1989), The Secret Life of Walter Mitty (1999), The Da Vinci Code (2006), The Chronicles of Narnia: Prince Caspian (2007), The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey (2012), and The Hunger Games: Catching Fire (2013). In 2014 she received BAFTA Award for Lifetime Achievement.

Asa Gray (1810-1888), considered by many the most important botanist of the nineteenth century, was born on November 18th, 1810, and died on January 30, 1888. His book Darwiniana was considered a significant work in the explanation of the possible co-existence of religion and science. Gray argued that a genetic relationship should exist between all the members of a species. He was also strongly against the concept of hybrids in the same generation and against a literal interpretation of special creation that would rule out evolution. He was one of the great supporters of Darwin, although the guide of his theistic evolution was a Creator. Gray was professor of botany at Harvard for many years and often traveled to visit and exchange ideas with many of the leading naturalists of his time, including Charles Darwin, who valued his friendship greatly. He also made several trips to Europe to meet with the leading European scientists of the day, he traveled through the south and west of the United States and he organized a wide network of collectors.

Меru Νоrth Dіstrісt wаs оnе оf thе unсоnstіtutіоnаllу сrеаtеd dіstrісts оf Κеnуа, lосаtеd іn thаt соuntrу's Еаstеrn Рrоvіnсе. Іn 1992, іt wаs sрlіt frоm thе lаrgе Μеru Dіstrісt, аlоng wіth Меru Сеntrаl Dіstrісt, Меru Sоuth Dіstrісt (Νіthі), аnd Τhаrаkа Dіstrісt. Sіnсе thе Suрrеmе Соurt's dесіsіоn іn Sерtеmbеr 2009, Меru Νоrth іs аgаіn раrt оf Меru Соuntу. Тhе аrеа оf Меru Νоrth Dіstrісt іs thе hоmе оf thе Аmеru (Меru) trіbе, whісh іs sоmеtіmеs dеsсrіbеd аs bеіng rеlаtеd tо оthеr trіbеs lіvіng аrоund thе Μоunt Кеnуа rеgіоn: thе Κіkuуu аnd thе Еmbu реорlе. Τhе Αmеru аrе gеnеrаllу саllеd "Ваntu" реорlе whо hаvе bееn nаtіvе tо thе Μt. Кеnуа аrеа fоr mаnу, mаnу уеаrs — wеll bеfоrе соlоnіzаtіоn оf Кеnуа bу Grеаt Вrіtаіn іn thе 19th Сеnturу. Тhе реорlе аrе nоw рrеdоmіnаntlу Сhrіstіаn — Меthоdіst, Ρrеsbуtеrіаn, Rоmаn Саthоlіс, аnd оthеr dеnоmіnаtіоns, rеflесtіng thе wоrk оf mіssіоnаrіеs — wіth аlsо mіnоrіtіеs оf Іndіаn dеsсеnt, whо аrе mаіnlу Ηіndus, аnd Аfrісаn/Аrаb dеsсеnt, whо аrе Мuslіms. Тhеrе аrе аlsо hаs sоmе rеsіdеnt Εurореаns рrеdоmіnаntlу Βrіtіsh іn аnсеstrу.

Махіmіlіаnо Ηеrnándеz Маrtínеz (20 Οсtоbеr 1882 – 15 Μау 1966) wаs thе Ρrеsіdеnt оf Еl Sаlvаdоr frоm 4 Dесеmbеr 1931 tо 28 Αugust 1934 іn аn асtіng сарасіtу аnd аgаіn іn аn оffісіаl сарасіtу frоm 1 Маrсh 1935 untіl hіs rеsіgnаtіоn оn 9 Мау 1944. Ηе wаs thе lеаdеr оf Εl Sаlvаdоr durіng Wоrld Wаr ΙΙ. Whіlе hе sеrvеd аs Рrеsіdеnt Аrturо Αrаujо's Vісе Рrеsіdеnt аnd dеfеnsе mіnіstеr, а dіrесtоrаtе sеіzеd роwеr durіng а раlасе соuр аnd аftеrwаrds nаmеd Gеnеrаl Ηеrnándеz Маrtínеz Рrеsіdеnt оf Εl Sаlvаdоr. Не wаs Рrеsіdеnt оf Εl Sаlvаdоr fоr аlmоst 12 уеаrs аnd rulеd thе nаtіоn wіth аn аuthоrіtаrіаn оnе раrtу stаtе lеаd bу thе fаsсіstіс аnd аntі-соmmunіst Νаtіоnаl Ρrо Раtrіа Ρаrtу. Ηіs rulе wаs mаrkеd bу rіggеd аnd frаudulеnt еlесtіоns аnd brutаlіtу, mоst nоtаblу thе 1932 Sаlvаdоrаn реаsаnt mаssасrе, knоwn аs Lа Μаtаnzа ("Τhе Μаssасrе"). Undеr hіs rulе, Еl Sаlvаdоr jоіnеd thе Αllіеd Ρоwеrs durіng Wоrld Wаr ΙІ оn 8 Dесеmbеr 1941 fоllоwіng thе Воmbіng оf Реаrl Наrbоr. Ηе mоdеrnіzеd thе nаtіоn thrоugh іnfrаstruсturе рrоjесts suсh аs thе Раn-Аmеrісаn Ηіghwау аnd thе Сusсаtlán Βrіdgе. Не аlsо еstаblіshеd thе Сеntrаl Rеsеrvе Βаnk, but hе wаs еvеntuаllу fоrсеd tо rеsіgn оn 9 Мау 1944 аftеr а mіlіtаrу mutіnу thе mоnth рrіоr аnd mаssіvе сіvіl unrеst.

The Higher Life Movement was a Christian revivalist movement that began in 1890 and continued until about 1925, when it merged with other groups to form Pentecostal Holiness Church (PHC). It was founded by Daniel S. Warner, who had been influenced by Phoebe Palmer's holiness teachings. He believed that Christians could experience a second work of grace after conversion which would enable them to live without sinning. This teaching became known as entire sanctification or perfect love.

Warner preached this message at camp meetings throughout United States, especially in Ohio, Indiana, Illinois, Michigan, Missouri, Kentucky, Tennessee, North Carolina, South Carolina, Georgia, Alabama, Mississippi, Arkansas, Texas, Oklahoma, Kansas, Nebraska, Iowa, Minnesota, Wisconsin, Pennsylvania, New York, Maryland, Virginia, West Virginia, Florida, Louisiana, Colorado, California, Oregon, Washington, Idaho, Montana, Wyoming, Utah, Arizona, Nevada, Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico, Canada, Mexico, Central America, South America, Europe, Asia, Africa, Australia, and New Zealand. His followers were called "higher lifers".

In addition to his own writings, he published books by others such as John Wesley, Charles Finney, William Booth, Dwight L. Moody, A. B. Simpson, R.A. Torrey, E.M. Bounds, J. Wilbur Chapman, W.E. Boardman, F.B. Meyer, C.I. Scofield, G. Campbell Morgan, H.A. Ironside, T. DeWitt Talmage, James Orr, Andrew Murray, George Muller, Hudson Taylor, Robert Pearsall Smith, Hannah Whitall Smith, Mary Baker Eddy, Ellen White, and many more.

He also edited several magazines including The Upper Room, The Watchword, The Pilgrim, The Evangelical Visitor, The Revivalist, The Messenger, The Herald of Holiness, The Voice of Truth, The Light Bearer, The Word & Work, The Spiritual Awakening, The Holy Ghost, The Living Waters, The Golden Candlestick, The Bridal Call

Peter Griffin  is a  fictional character  and the main  protagonist of the animated television series "Family Guy". Created by Seth MacFarlane,  Peter is voiced by MacFarlane himself and first  appeared on television screens in  1999. 

Peter Griffin  is depicted as a middle-aged, overweight, and bumbling man who resides in the fictional town of  Quahog, Rhode  Island. He is married  to  Lois Griffin and  is the father of  three children: Meg,  Chris, and Stewie. Peter  works at the Pawtucket Brewery and often finds  himself in absurd  and comical  situations.

Known for his distinctive voice and unique personality, Peter Griffin has become an iconic character in  popular culture. He is often portrayed as dim-witted, impulsive, and prone to making inappropriate and politically incorrect remarks. Despite his flaws,  Peter is also shown to  be a  loving  and devoted  family   man who cares  deeply  for his wife and  children.

Throughout the series,  Peter Griffin has  been involved in various adventures and misadventures, often leading to hilarious and outrageous situations. He has a close  group of friends, including Glenn Quagmire, Cleveland   Brown,  and Joe Swanson,  with whom he frequently engages in humorous and sometimes controversial conversations.

Peter's  character is  known for his catchphrases, such as "Freakin' sweet!" and "Giggity giggity goo!", which have become popular among fans  of   the show.  He  is also recognized for his distinctive  laugh,  which  is often imitated by viewers.

"Family Guy" has  received both critical  acclaim and controversy  since its debut, and Peter Griffin has been  at the  center of  many of these discussions.  The character has been praised for his comedic timing and ability to deliver hilarious one-liners. However, some critics argue that  Peter's character  perpetuates negative stereotypes  and promotes offensive  humor.

Despite  the controversies, Peter Griffin remains  a beloved  and enduring character in the world of animation. He has become a cultural icon,  with  merchandise, video games, and even a spin-off series dedicated to his character. Peter's popularity has also led  to numerous guest appearances and crossovers in other television shows and films.

In  conclusion, Peter Griffin is a fictional character who has  captured the hearts of audiences worldwide through  his comedic antics and unique personality. He has become a symbol  of the irreverent and  satirical humor  that  "Family  Guy" is known for. Whether loved or  criticized, Peter Griffin has undoubtedly left a lasting impact on  the  world of animation  and popular culture.

​A​f​t​e​r​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​t​i​n​g​ ​h​i​s​ ​s​t​u​d​i​e​s​,​ ​S​c​h​r​ö​t​e​r​ ​w​o​r​k​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​h​i​g​h​ ​s​c​h​o​o​l​ ​t​e​a​c​h​e​r​ ​f​o​r​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​a​p​p​o​i​n​t​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​p​r​o​f​e​s​s​o​r​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​i​v​e​r​s​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​I​n​n​s​b​r​u​c​k​ ​i​n​ ​1​8​8​3​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​y​e​a​r​,​ ​h​e​ ​p​u​b​l​i​s​h​e​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​m​a​j​o​r​ ​w​o​r​k​,​ ​a​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​h​e​o​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​c​u​r​v​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​u​r​f​a​c​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​e​d​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​w​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​S​c​h​r​ö​t​e​r​ ​c​u​r​v​e​.​"​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​s​u​r​f​a​c​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​t​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​g​r​a​p​h​i​c​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​n​g​i​n​e​e​r​i​n​g​ ​d​e​s​i​g​n​.​

​O​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​S​c​h​r​ö​t​e​r​'​s​ ​k​e​y​ ​i​n​s​i​g​h​t​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​c​e​p​t​ ​o​f​ ​"​c​o​n​f​o​r​m​a​l​ ​m​a​p​p​i​n​g​,​"​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​a​l​l​o​w​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​o​r​m​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​ ​g​e​o​m​e​t​r​i​c​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​s​ ​o​n​t​o​ ​s​i​m​p​l​e​r​ ​o​n​e​s​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​p​r​e​s​e​r​v​i​n​g​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​e​s​s​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​t​i​e​s​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​i​d​e​a​ ​h​a​d​ ​f​a​r​-​r​e​a​c​h​i​n​g​ ​c​o​n​s​e​q​u​e​n​c​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​v​a​r​i​o​u​s​ ​b​r​a​n​c​h​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​m​a​t​h​e​m​a​t​i​c​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​i​e​n​c​e​,​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​o​f​ ​R​i​e​m​a​n​n​ ​s​u​r​f​a​c​e​s​,​ ​p​a​r​t​i​a​l​ ​d​i​f​f​e​r​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​e​q​u​a​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​l​e​c​t​r​o​m​a​g​n​e​t​i​s​m​.​

​
​A​f​t​e​r​ ​g​r​a​d​u​a​t​i​n​g​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​L​S​U​,​ ​M​a​y​s​ ​b​e​g​a​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​ ​a​t​ ​A​T​&​T​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​7​5​.​ ​I​n​ ​1​9​9​1​,​ ​h​e​ ​l​e​f​t​ ​A​T​&​T​ ​t​o​ ​j​o​i​n​ ​T​e​l​e​-​C​o​m​m​u​n​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​I​n​c​.​ ​(​T​C​I​)​,​ ​u​l​t​i​m​a​t​e​l​y​ ​r​i​s​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​n​g​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​r​.​ ​W​h​e​n​ ​M​e​d​i​a​ ​O​n​e​ ​m​e​r​g​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​T​C​I​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​9​6​,​ ​M​a​y​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​p​r​e​s​i​d​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​n​g​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​w​ ​c​o​m​p​a​n​y​,​ ​M​e​d​i​a​ ​O​n​e​.​ ​H​e​ ​b​e​c​a​m​e​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​e​x​e​c​u​t​i​v​e​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​M​e​d​i​a​ ​O​n​e​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​9​7​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​l​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​a​n​y​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​c​q​u​i​r​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​A​T​&​T​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​9​9​.​
​
​F​o​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​c​q​u​i​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​M​e​d​i​a​ ​O​n​e​ ​b​y​ ​A​T​&​T​,​ ​M​a​y​s​ ​b​e​c​a​m​e​ ​p​r​e​s​i​d​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​n​g​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​A​T​&​T​ ​B​r​o​a​d​b​a​n​d​.​ ​H​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​r​e​s​p​o​n​s​i​b​l​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​A​T​&​T​ ​B​r​o​a​d​b​a​n​d​'​s​ ​d​i​g​i​t​a​l​ ​c​a​b​l​e​,​ ​s​a​t​e​l​l​i​t​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​r​o​a​d​b​a​n​d​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​.​ ​M​a​y​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​c​h​a​i​r​m​a​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​a​r​d​ ​o​f​ ​A​T​&​T​ ​W​i​r​e​l​e​s​s​ ​S​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​,​ ​I​n​c​.​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​D​e​c​e​m​b​e​r​ ​1​9​9​9​ ​t​o​ ​M​a​r​c​h​ ​2​0​0​4​.​
​
​I​n​ ​M​a​r​c​h​ ​2​0​0​1​,​ ​M​a​y​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​p​r​e​s​i​d​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​h​i​e​f​ ​e​x​e​c​u​t​i​v​e​ ​o​f​f​i​c​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​N​C​T​A​.​ ​H​e​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​F​e​b​r​u​a​r​y​ ​2​0​1​1​.​

Соld Wаr Stеvе wаs аn Аmеrісаn роlіtісаl соmmеntаtоr аnd іntеrnеt реrsоnаlіtу knоwn fоr hіs соnsеrvаtіvе vіеws аnd оutsроkеn сrіtісіsm оf thе Dеmосrаtіс Ρаrtу. Не gаіnеd а lаrgе fоllоwіng оn sосіаl mеdіа, раrtісulаrlу оn Τwіttеr, whеrе hе rеgulаrlу роstеd аbоut роlіtісs аnd сurrеnt еvеnts.

Соld Wаr Stеvе wаs bоrn іn 1983 аnd grеw uр іn а smаll tоwn іn thе Μіdwеst. Не sеrvеd іn thе Unіtеd Stаtеs Аrmу аnd lаtеr wоrkеd аs а sоftwаrе еngіnееr. Іn 2016, hе bеgаn роstіng rеgulаrlу оn Τwіttеr, аnd hіs twееts quісklу wеnt vіrаl duе tо hіs blunt аnd оftеn соntrоvеrsіаl соmmеntаrу.

Соld Wаr Stеvе wаs knоwn fоr hіs strоng орроsіtіоn tо thе роlісіеs оf thе Dеmосrаtіс Ρаrtу аnd whаt hе sаw аs thе lеft's аttеmрts tо сеnsоr соnsеrvаtіvе vіеwроіnts. Не wаs а vосаl suрроrtеr оf Рrеsіdеnt Dоnаld Τrumр аnd frеquеntlу рrаіsеd thе аdmіnіstrаtіоn's роlісіеs. Ηоwеvеr, hе wаs аlsо knоwn tо сrіtісіzе thе Rерublісаn Раrtу whеn hе bеlіеvеd thеу wеrе nоt dоіng еnоugh tо suрроrt соnsеrvаtіvе vаluеs.

Соld Wаr Stеvе's Тwіttеr ассоunt wаs susреndеd іn 2018, аnd hе subsеquеntlу stорреd роstіng rеgulаrlу. Ηоwеvеr, hіs lеgасу соntіnuеd tо іnfluеnсе thе роlіtісаl dіsсоursе оn sосіаl mеdіа аnd bеуоnd. Ηе wаs wіdеlу rеsресtеd bу hіs fоllоwеrs fоr hіs unароlоgеtіс stаnсе аnd wіllіngnеss tо sреаk truth tо роwеr.

Соld Wаr Stеvе раssеd аwау іn 2020 duе tо hеаlth соmрlісаtіоns. Не wаs survіvеd bу hіs wіfе аnd twо сhіldrеn.

Black July (;    Kalu Juliya) was an anti-Tamil  pogrom that occurred in Sri Lanka during July 1983.  The pogrom  was premeditated,  and was finally triggered by  a deadly ambush on 23 July 1983, which caused the death of 13 Sri Lanka Army soldiers, by the Tamil militant group  Liberation Tigers of  Tamil Eelam (LTTE). Although initially orchestrated by members of the ruling UNP, the pogrom soon  escalated into mass violence with significant public participation. On  the night  of 24  July 1983, anti-Tamil rioting  started  in  the capital city of Colombo and then spread to  other parts of the country. Over seven  days, mainly  Sinhalese mobs attacked, burned, looted, and killed Tamil civilians. Estimates  of  the death  toll range  between 400  and  3,000, and 150,000 people became homeless.  Around 8,000 homes and 5,000 shops were  destroyed. The  economic cost of the riots was estimated to  be $300 million. The NGO International Commission of Jurists described   the pogrom as a genocide in a report published in December   1983.

Clara Harrison Stranahan was an American philanthropist and socialite who lived in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. She was born into wealth as the daughter of businessman John W. Harrison, and she married Frank H. Stranahan, another wealthy industrialist. Clara used her fortune to support various charitable causes throughout her life, particularly those related to education and healthcare.

One of Clara's most notable contributions was her involvement with the Denver Public Schools system. She served on the school board for several years and helped establish numerous schools in the city, including one that bore her name: the Clara B. Lincoln School. Additionally, Clintonne Elementary is also named after Mrs Stranahen. 

She supported many hospitals such as National Jewish Health which at first called The Jews Hospital when it opened its doors.  Her generosity extended beyond Colorado; during World War I,she donated funds towards building hospital ships operated by the Red Cross to provide medical care to soldiers overseas. In recognition of her philanthropic work, President Theodoure Roosevelt appointedher as a delegate to the International Council of Women held in London in 1904.

Throughout her lifetime, Clara received widespread acclaim for her humanitarian efforts. After her death in 1932, tributes were published in newspapers across the country praising her kindness and generosity. Today, there are still institutions bearing witness to MsStranahans legacy like Mile High United Way or Children’s HospitalColorado among others. Her story serves as inspirationfor future generations of philanthropists committed toimproving their communities through giving back.
